notes: Spec asserts: parent ready→working at spawn, child session appears with ParentSessionID=parent.session_id, SubagentCount(parent)=1 while child alive, parent stays working until child ends, parent working→ready after child ends. The recipe drives a single Task-tool call from the parent so claudecode emits an Agent tool_use, the fswatcher discovers the sidechain transcript under <parent>/subagents/, and the detector emits KindParentLinked binding child→parent (parser.go session_detector_activity.go:110). The parent_link phase asserts the parent_linked lifecycle event; the intermediate_working phase pins the parent to working across the child's lifetime. Top-level verify also asserts parent_linked_min:1 and subagent_transcripts_min:1, matching the existing orchestrator-side subagent-spawn recipe shape.

text: User prompt drives parent ready → working (spec bullet 1)
Subagent transcript materializes under <parent>/subagents/ — fswatcher discovers a new SessionState for the child
Detector emits parent_linked binding child.session_id → parent.session_id (spec bullet 2: ParentSessionID = parent.session_id)

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while child is alive — outstanding Agent tool_use blocks IsAgentDone (spec bullet 4)
After the child reports back (task-notification) the parent's terminal stop_reason fires: working → ready (spec bullet 5)
